name: Soto del Barco, Castillo de San Martín, Río Nalón, Muros de Nalón, Viaducto Concha de Artero
name_original: Soto del Barco, Castillo de San Martín, Río Nalón, Muros de Nalón, Viaducto Concha de Artero – circular
description: This difficult 34.3-mile mountain bike route explores the Asturian coast and Nalón River, linking historic castles, charming towns, and scen
region_intro: Mountain biking around Soto Del Barco offers diverse landscapes, from the Nalón River estuary to the Cantabrian coastline. The terrain features a blend of gentle hills and more pronounced elevations in areas like the Sierra de Mafalla and Fontebona. This region provides a varied backdrop for mountain bikers, with routes traversing coastal paths, riverside trails, and forested areas. The mild, temperate climate typical of the Cantabrian coast supports year-round outdoor activity. Best mountain bike trails in Soto Del Barco The most popular mountain bike trail is Soto del Barco, Castillo de San Martín, Río Nalón, Muros de Nalón, Viaducto Concha de Artero, a difficult 34.3 miles (55.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 20 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Nalón River and historical landmarks. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Pravia, Playa el Garruncho, La Quinta de Selgas en El Pitu, Cudillero – ruta circular, a difficult 32.3 miles (52.0 km) path. This circular route explores coastal areas and cultural sites. Local mountain bikers also love the Stream Crossing loop from Santiagu'l Monte, a 22.4 miles (36.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Mountain biking around Soto Del Barco is defined by its river estuary, Cantabrian coastline, and gentle hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Soto Del Barco are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 700 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Soto Del Barco's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Soto Del Barco?
A: There are over 60 mountain bike trails available around Soto Del Barco, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 700 times.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Soto Del Barco?
A: Yes, Soto Del Barco offers a few easier routes, with 2 trails specifically categorized as easy. While the region's terrain includes gentle hills, some routes can be quite challenging. For a family-friendly experience, look for routes with minimal elevation gain and shorter distances, often found along the Nalón River estuary or coastal paths.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Soto Del Barco?
A: The terrain around Soto Del Barco is diverse, ranging from the flat, scenic paths along the Nalón River estuary and the Cantabrian coastline to more pronounced hills in the southern parts, such as the Sierra de Mafalla and Fontebona. You'll encounter a mix of coastal, riverside, and forested environments, providing varied backdrops for your rides.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027484814/pravia-playa-el-garruncho-la-quinta-de-selgas-en-el-pitu-cudillero-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pravia, Playa el Garruncho, La Quinta de Selgas en El Pitu, Cudillero – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 32.3-mile (52.0 km) loop that explores coastal areas and cultural sites. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40310592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stream Crossing loop from Santiagu'l Monte&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 22.4-mile (36.1 km) trail.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I can see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails offer views of historical and natural landmarks. You might pass by the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de San Martín&lt;/a&gt; overlooking the Nalón River estuary, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2005855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Concha de Artedo Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the elegant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Selgas Palace and the Jesús Nazareno Church‑Pantheon&lt;/a&gt;, known for its art and gardens, and fine examples of Indiano architecture.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Soto Del Barco?
A: Soto Del Barco benefits from a mild, temperate, and humid climate typical of the Cantabrian coast, making it suitable for mountain biking year-round. However, spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant conditions with moderate temperatures and less rainfall, ideal for exploring both coastal and inland routes.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Soto Del Barco features 35 trails categorized as difficult, providing ample challenge for experienced mountain bikers. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027485210/soto-del-barco-castillo-de-san-martin-rio-nalon-muros-de-nalon-viaducto-concha-de-artero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soto del Barco, Castillo de San Martín, Río Nalón, Muros de Nalón, Viaducto Concha de Artero&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 34.3-mile (55.2 km) route with significant elevation gain.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Soto Del Barco?
A: The mountain bike trails in Soto Del Barco are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, which combine river estuaries, the Cantabrian coastline, and gentle hills, offering varied and scenic experiences.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Soto Del Barco, particularly in towns and near popular trailheads. Specific parking information can often be found in the details of individual komoot tours, helping you plan your starting point.

Q: Can I reach mountain bike trails using public transport in Soto Del Barco?
A: While some areas of Soto Del Barco are accessible by public transport, reaching specific trailheads might require some planning. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or train connections to nearby towns like San Juan de la Arena or Pravia, and then assess the distance to your desired starting point.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass by pubs or cafes?
A: Many routes in Soto Del Barco will take you through or near charming towns and villages, such as San Juan de la Arena, where you can find local cafes and pubs. The area's fishing towns and cultural centers offer opportunities to stop for refreshments and experience local Asturian hospitality.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the mountain bike routes in Soto Del Barco?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on many outdoor trails in the region, especially if kept on a leash. However, it's always best to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas to ensure your furry companion is welcome. The diverse natural environment offers plenty of space for you and your dog to enjoy.
tips:
The Castillo de San Martín, in the council of Soto del Barco, is located on the right bank of the river Nalón, on a pronounced bend from which the channel opens towards the estuary of San Esteban de Pravia. Its location, on a promontory barely 40 m high, provides it with an excellent visual domain over the mouth of the most important river artery in the region and justifies its long history as a fortification.
The current castle was built by Alfonso III on the remains of a previous settlement, in order to protect the coast and the estuary from Norman incursions.
The Nalón River, without counting the Narcea, has the largest basin area (3,692 km2) and is the longest (145 km) and mightiest river in Asturias.

A small coastal municipality of just over 1,800 inhabitants. It is worth seeing the stately town of Muros (of Roman and medieval origin), the walls of the old Palace of Valdecarzana and the Palace of the Marqués de Muros, and its old mansions and beautiful buildings.

The Quinta de Selgas is a 19th century palace built on the initiative of the brothers Ezequiel and Fortunato de Selgas Albuerne. Those who wanted to have a palace in the Asturian lands in the Italian way and endow it with important artistic pieces, furniture and household items from renowned houses. Its collection includes pieces by Goya and El Greco. It also has an outstanding collection of tapestries, porcelain and glassware. The entire property is surrounded by a wall and has its own church and a beautifully landscaped garden.

Between Las Dueñas and Muros de Nalón we come across this beautiful 1,184 meter long viaduct that crosses the valleys formed by the Uncín and Especuero rivers. Shell-shaped, this viaduct favors cyclists thanks to the reduction in traffic on this road.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike route is approximately 55.2 kilometers long and typically takes around 5 hours and 20 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult. While some sections along the river or coast might be easier, the overall route connecting these points in Asturias would likely involve significant elevation gains and varied terrain, making it more suitable for experienced mountain bikers.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This is designed as a circular mountain bike route, allowing you to start and end in the same general area after exploring the various landmarks.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: While there isn't one formally designated trailhead for this specific circular route, you could look for parking in towns like Soto del Barco or Muros de Nalón. These towns offer good starting points with access to the local road networks and proximity to the river and coastal paths.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You can expect paths along the Río Nalón that might be compacted gravel or natural singletrack, potentially muddy after rain. Further inland, especially around Soto del Barco, the route could include undulating forest tracks and some paved sections, with continuous ascents and descents.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable landmarks. These include the historic Castillo de San Martín, the scenic Nalón River, the charming settlement of Muros de Nalón, and the impressive Concha de Artedo Viaduct. You might also pass by the Quinta de Selgas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time for mountain biking in Asturias, including this area, is generally during the spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October). The weather is usually milder, and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can also be good, but be prepared for potentially higher temperatures. Winter can bring rain and colder conditions, affecting trail surfaces.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, many trails in Asturias are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through towns, near livestock, or on busier sections. Be mindful of other trail users and local regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to access the natural areas and trails along this route in Asturias. However, if you visit specific historical sites or attractions, they might have their own entry fees.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other recognized paths. You will find it intersects with parts of the Northern Route (Asturia), Mtb Northern Route (Asturia), Camino del Norte, and Senda Norte, L'Arena-El Castillu.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing through towns like Soto del Barco and Muros de Nalón offers opportunities to find local cafes, pubs, and accommodation. These towns are good spots to refuel and experience local Asturian hospitality.
